meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Learn how to use idea validation to overcome debugging hurdles. An intermediate tutorial for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to build faster.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about syntax; it's about architectural clarity. When you can't debug, your idea is likely over-scoped. Use idea validation to strip complexity, isolate core logic, and use AI-assisted agents like Claude Code to refactor non-functional features, allowing you to focus on the product's value proposition rather than technical debt.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal flickering with red error logs, the silence of your room amplified by the crushing weight of a project that won't compile. The ambition that fueled your late-night coding session has hit a wall, and the code you thought was elegant now feels like a labyrinth of bugs. You are not alone; every senior engineer has stood exactly where you are, feeling the disconnect between a grand vision and a broken reality.

But here is the industry secret: most debugging nightmares are simply symptoms of poor idea validation. When the logic becomes too complex to track, you aren't just fighting code—you are fighting a product roadmap that outpaced your current skill set. By pivoting back to idea validation, you can simplify your scope, isolate your core features, and regain the momentum required to ship your first production-grade app.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Feature
steps.description: Strip your project down to the one core action that provides value. If it doesn't serve that action, delete it for now to reduce surface area for bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and identify the core value proposition. Suggest a minimal subset of files that represent the primary feature, ignoring all secondary UI/UX bloat.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify the core feature and list files required for a minimal proof of concept'

steps.title: Isolate Logic with Stubbing
steps.description: Use stubs to replace high-complexity external dependencies. This ensures that the bugs you are chasing are in your logic, not your third-party integrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock version of the current database/API integration to isolate the business logic from external service failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'replace external API calls with mock data structures to isolate core logic'

steps.title: Refactor for Testability
steps.description: Transform your monolithic functions into modular, pure functions. This makes it impossible for bugs to hide in side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function into smaller, pure functions that accept inputs and return outputs without modifying global state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file=core_logic.py --prompt='refactor into pure functions for better unit testing'

steps.title: Automated Regression Audit
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate a suite of unit tests based on your validated logic to catch future regressions before they happen.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for the current logic using pytest that covers edge cases for the identified core feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for the core logic and run them to ensure stability'

steps.title: Continuous Validation Loop
steps.description: Set up a feedback loop where every new feature must pass the core logic audit before being integrated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist for verifying that new code additions do not violate the core business logic established in the previous steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a pre-commit check script to validate core logic against new changes'

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as data, not failure; use AI to categorize errors by frequency to identify which architectural component is the 'weakest link'.
If you spend more than 30 minutes debugging a single function, delete it and rewrite it from scratch using TDD (Test Driven Development).
Use Claude Code to explain your code back to you; if the AI's explanation doesn't match your intent, the bug is in your logic, not the syntax.
Always maintain a 'Sandbox' branch where you can break things without touching your stable, validated core logic.
Document the 'Why' of your code, not the 'How'; when you return to a project weeks later, the intent is the first thing you'll forget.
content.next_steps: Once your core logic is stable, start building out your UI layer one component at a time, keeping the validation loop active to ensure your app stays performant.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the methodology to stop fighting your code and start building your legacy. Stop debugging in the dark and start building with purpose. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
